summary: HNSW is functional-but-not-leading on the current remote x86 authority lane: recall and production contract gates are closed, but native remains about 14.8x faster on the trusted same-schema HDF5 benchmark.
action: Archive HNSW as functional-but-not-leading, allow production-contract usage to rely on the closed FFI/persistence evidence, and keep HNSW out of performance-leadership claims until a new remote authority artifact materially closes the native throughput gap.
